claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 10 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're asking about.

Let me start with: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me:
- How many children you're asking about?
- Their names (or you can use initials if you prefer)?
- Their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

I have one child I'm asking about. His name is Alex and he's 10 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Alex is 10 years old, which means he's under 16, so that's a good start.

Now I need to ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

**Does Alex normally live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me. He lives with his mum.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. This is an important factor in determining eligibility.

**Can you tell me - does anyone else currently receive Child Benefit for Alex, or are you aware if his mum is claiming Child Benefit for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, his mum is already claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. This is a key piece of information.

I have one more important question: **Do you contribute towards Alex's upkeep?** For example, do you:
- Pay maintenance or child support for him?
- Contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit would pay (currently £25.60 per week for the eldest/only child or £16.95 per week for additional children)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards Alex's upkeep. I help out with things like clothes and bits and pieces for school - I give about £20 a week.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me, I need to make my determination according to the UK Child Benefit rules.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## My Assessment

I'm sorry, but **you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex** based on your current circumstances.

Here's why:

**For Alex (age 10):**
- While Alex meets the age requirement (he's under 16), the key issue is that **only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any time**
- Alex's mother is already claiming Child Benefit for him
- Even though you contribute £20 per week towards Alex's upkeep, you cannot claim Child Benefit for him while his mother is receiving it

**What this means:**
You would only be able to claim Child Benefit for Alex if his mother stopped claiming it. If there were ever a change in circumstances where she was no longer claiming (or if Alex came to live with you primarily), you could potentially make a claim at that point.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or your situation?

reasonings: Alex is 10 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, Alex does not live with you - he lives with his mother. According to UK Child Benefit rules, only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at any time. Alex's mother is already claiming Child Benefit for him. While you do contribute towards Alex's upkeep (£20 per week), this does not override the fact that his mother is already the claimant. You cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex while his mother is receiving it for him, even if you contribute to his costs.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 10), who lives with his mother. The mother is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Under UK rules, only one person can receive Child Benefit for a child at any given time, so the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex while the mother's claim is active.

While you do contribute towards Alex's upkeep (£20 per week), this does not override the fact that his mother is already the claimant. You cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex while his mother is receiving it for him, even if you contribute to his costs.

Under UK rules, only one person can receive Child Benefit for a child at any given time, so the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex while the mother's claim is active.
